hashlib/hash/name.name: name
hashlib/hash/name.description: The canonical name of this hash, always lowercase and always suitable as a parameter to new() to create another hash of this type. Changed in version 3.4: The name attribute has been present in CPython since its inception, but until Python 3.4 was not formally specified, so may not exist on some platforms.

hashlib/new.name: new
hashlib/new.description: Is a generic constructor that takes the string name of the desired algorithm as its first parameter. It also exists to allow access to the above listed hashes as well as any other algorithms that your OpenSSL library may offer.
